titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Records of Mission and World Christian Educational and Medical Institutions and Organizations
Records of Mission and World Christian Educational and Medical Institutions and Organizations
titlestmt.author: Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 278 digital files.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 28, 2026 at 18:00

did.unittitle: Records of Mission and World Christian Educational and Medical Institutions and Organizations
did.abstract: This collection draws together documentation relating to educational and medical institutions and organizations that were established by mission agencies and World Christian churches. It complements other substantial archival collections held by the Divinity Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into four series:
Series II. Educational Associations and Coordinating Organizations
Series IV. Medical Associations and Coordinating Organizations

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Educational and medical work have been integral parts of the missionary movement and churches formed from that movement. Throughout the world, mission agencies and churches established schools, hospitals, and centers for the treatment of individuals suffering from leprosy (Hansen's disease), mental illness, and blindness.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Much of this collection consists of printed or mimeographed reports, memos, brochures and other documentation of the work of the educational and medical institutions and organizations represented. This material came to the Divinity Library in various ways over the years. The documentation in this collection is not comprehensive but it provides useful background and detail about educational and medical work of mission agencies and the churches formed out of the mission movement, from its beginnings until the present time. The majority of the material in this collection dates from the 19th and 20th centuries. It should be noted that extensive additional information related to educational and medical work can be found in reports and publications of the mission organizations in Record Group 349, Records of Mission and World Christian Agencies and Organizations, and in the Library's cataloged collection.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Records of Mission and World Christian Educational and Medical Institutions and Organizations (RG 347), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletter of general information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual bulletin, 1957-1958.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prospectus of first school in Kenya offering secondary education for boys, established in 1926 after an agreement for conjoint operation between the Alliance of Protestant Missions and the British Colonial Government.

did.unittitle: Catholic Higher Education Institute of Eastern Africa, Nairobi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informational brochure. CHIEA opened its Faculty of Theology with 21 students on September 3, 1984 and was formally inaugurated by Pope John Paul II on August 18, 1985. Its chief objectives were to become a fully-fledged Catholic university, to become a center of research and publications in theology and related subjects, and to advance ecumenism through dialogue with other Christian churches.

did.unittitle: Tanzaga College, Catholic University of Eastern Africa, Nairobi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Academic handbook 2005-2006. Tangaza College (originally Tangaza Theological Centre) is "a centre of Catholic theological and ministerial education in the context of the Church in Africa". It is a constituent college of the Catholic University of Eastern Africa, but remains administratively autonomous.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Principal's inaugural address.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters, annual report.

scopecontent.p: Newsletters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual report.

scopecontent.p: Prospectus of school located in Nanjing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications describe the work of the Christian Herald Industrial Mission, which was located in Fuzhou and provided care and technical education for orphaned children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prospectus for seminary that was located in Chongqing and was associated with the China Inland Mission.

did.unittitle: College of Chinese Studies [Zhongguo wen hua xue yuan]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Course catalogs and other documentation of the College of Chinese Studies, which was located in Beijing and continued the work of the North China Union Language School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Emergency College was established in Shanghai to provide educational opportunities for missionaries who had been evacuated from interior China due to unsettled political conditions in 1926-1927.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Foochow College was established in Fuzhou and moved temporarily to Ingtai [Yongtai], Fujian during the Sino-Japanese War.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Catalogue and magazine issue of school located in Changsha.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Home had Chinese leadership and provided technical education, including creation of lacquerware.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters from Alice Margaret Huggins describing the work of the school, which was located in Tunghsien, near Beijing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports for school located in Quangzhou.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of Executive Committee in Cambridge, US regarding school located in Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of the Board of Directors of the school, which was located in Hangzhou.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Hsiang Ya Medical College [Guo li Xiang Ya yi xue yuan]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Hua Chung College [Hua zhong shi fan ]xue yuan]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The College, located in Wuchang, Wuhan, was known as Central China Teachers College and Central China University. See additional records in the Yale Divinity Library's Record Group 11.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports, brochure for institution located in Shanghai. See also: Records of the Association for the Chinese Blind in Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memo describing the school, which was located in Tongzhou. a district of Beijing/

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article describing this school for technical education was located in Sichuan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure and newsletter describing Kuling School, a school for missionary children located in Guling, Jiangxi. For more documentation see Yale Divinity Library Record Group 209.

did.unittitle: Light Giving School for Blind Girls and Boys

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report for school located in Beijing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue of "Bell" magazine published by the school, which was located in Shanghai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bulletin and reports of seminary located in Shekow, Hubei.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 50th anniversary booklet of school located in Nanjing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ming Hsien was a school in Taigu, Shanxi, founded by Oberlin College graduate H.H. Kung in 1907 with support from the Oberlin Shansi Memorial Association. Material consists of a report from the Ming Hsien Executive Committee report on the school after the Communist liberation in 1950.

did.unittitle: Moukden Medical College [Fengtian yi ke da xue]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports for school supported by the Church of Scotland that was located in Mukden, now Shenyang, Liaoning Province.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure describing seminary and plans for construction of new campus that was started in 2005.

did.unittitle: Nanking Theological Seminary Board of Founders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Board of Founders of Nanking Theological Seminary [Jinling shen xue yuan] was founded in 1937. The original charter of the Nanking Theological Seminary was amended in 1952 to expand the purposes of the organization to include support of theological education in China and other parts of Asia. In 1963 the name of the corporation was changed to the Foundation for Theological Education in South East Asia. See additional documentation in Yale Divinity Library Record Group 180.

did.unittitle: Documents from conference on Translation of Christian Classics into Chinese

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brochure describing school located in Shanghai

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports,course catalogues, and photographs for North China Union College, which was located in Tongzhou, a district of Beijing. The school began as North China College and later merged with other schools to create Yenching University. For more documentation, see Yale Divinity Library Record Group 11.

did.unittitle: North China Theological Seminary [Hwa Bei Shen Hsueh]

did.unittitle: Woman's Christian Medical College (Shanghai). American Board of Founders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Board was formed in 1924. After evacuation of American missionaries from China in 1949, funds of Board were used for scholarships for Asian women in medical training. The Board voted to dissolve in 1974.

did.unittitle: Historical sketch of work; lists of Board members
Annual reports to Superintendent of Corporation, District of Columbia
Material regarding Bylaws, Certificate of Incorporation, Licensing
Material regarding insurance on buildings in Shanghai
Material regarding war damage claims against the Japanese government
Financial material, including record of origin of funds, bank records
Correspondence and reports of Field Treasurers in Shanghai
Miscellaneous Financial material; check stubs, stock records, etc.
Material related to formation, operations, alumnae and staff of Shanghai institutions; including photographs
Correspondence of individuals and agencies connected with WCMC
Seal and stamp of American Board of WCMC

scopecontent.p: Reports, schedules of lectures, histories for school founded 1903, under the auspices of the British Homoeopathic Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual reports for institution founded in 1886 as an non-sectarian training home for potential missionaries, located in Drayton Park, Highbury, London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bulletin for institute founded in 1949 as part of the Canadian School of Missions and that offered intensive language and culture courses to training missionaries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included in this collection are institutions that were established for mission outreach or for the training of missionaries. See Yale Divinity Library Record Group 33 for records of religious educational institutions focused on training for the ministry. See Record Group 349 under American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions for Oahu College and Hilo Boarding School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet describing the Melanesian Institute, which was founded in 1968 by the Association of Clerical Religious Superiors of the Catholic church in response to the need for new missionaries to have a greater knowledge and understanding of the people, culture and traditions of Melanesia. The first orientation course was held in 1969. Since 1971, the Anglican, Lutheran and United Churches have joined in the various courses and publications of the Institute.

did.unittitle: United Board for Christian Higher Education in Asia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organization previously known as the Associated Boards for Christian Colleges in China and the United Board for Christian Colleges in China. Extensive additional material is available in YDSL Record Group Nos. 11, 11A, 11B, 11C, 11D, 11E, 11F and 11G, Records of the United Board for Christian Higher Education in Asia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports and brochures for organization formerly known as the Institution for the Chinese Blind, Inc., affiliated with United China Relief, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The organization was headquartered in New York. It originally provided support for the Peking Union Medical College, but later extended its efforts to all countries in Asia. It provided grants for medical and nursing schools for visiting professorships, fellowships, books and journals for medical libraries, and equipment for laboratories.
